affected_services.details: up{job="frontend"} dropped to 0.0 at 16:14:51 UTC, indicating service crash or failure to start. HTTP 500 errors spiked to 3.02752 req/s at 16:53:06 UTC, and latency spiked to 0.03638s at 16:57:06 UTC.
285 DNS resolution failures (e.g., 'dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp ... i/o timeout') between 16:23:23–16:55:36 UTC.
Traces show /metrics endpoint latency spikes to 908ms (traceID: ea32b88d7d3e18d4c82193dff9da3263) and 617ms (traceID: dab1efc3f1b94242abf1b27a5cee7866).

root_cause.confidence_rationale: Root cause is supported by 3 independent signals (logs, metrics, traces) with no contradictions. Temporal coherence shows front-end failure preceded DNS resolution failures. Alternatives (Tempo/Loki resource exhaustion, orders degradation) were ruled out as they occurred after the front-end failure or lacked evidence for DNS dependency.
root_cause.summary: The front-end service failed to start due to a configuration error (SyntaxError and npm startup failure), causing it to crash or never initialize. This led to DNS resolution failures in edge-router and cascading HTTP 500 errors.

causal_chain.event: front-end fails to start due to SyntaxError and npm ERR!.
causal_chain.mechanism: Misconfigured startup script or environment variable.
causal_chain.evidence: [ERROR ×75] npm ERR! Failed at the microservices-demo-front-end@0.0.1 start script (front-end logs).
up{job="frontend"}: min=0.0, avg=0.70647, max=1.0 (peak at 16:14:51 UTC) (Metrics 05).

causal_chain.event: edge-router fails to resolve front-end via DNS (127.0.0.11:53).
causal_chain.mechanism: front-end not registered in DNS due to crash.
causal_chain.evidence: [ERROR ×40] dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp ... i/o timeout (edge-router logs).

causal_chain.mechanism: front-end cannot connect to downstream service.

causal_chain.event: First trace shows DNS resolution failed.
causal_chain.mechanism: edge-router cannot forward requests to front-end.
causal_chain.evidence: traceID: [redacted], error: DNS resolution failed (Trace Agent response).

causal_chain.mechanism: edge-router returns 500s due to unreachable front-end.
Retries and timeouts from edge-router increase latency.

contributing_factors: Missing instrumentation for DNS server (127.0.0.11:53) logs, preventing direct confirmation of DNS failures.
High memory usage in front-end (486 MB peak) may have exacerbated startup instability.
Orders service CPU throttling (22.8× spike) and latency spikes (908ms) occurred after front-end failures, amplifying user impact.
alternatives_considered: Tempo/Loki resource exhaustion: Ruled out due to lack of evidence linking DNS server (127.0.0.11:53) to Tempo/Loki.
Orders service degradation: Ruled out as orders latency spikes occurred after front-end failures and do not explain DNS resolution failures.
Front-end crash loop alone: Ruled out as DNS failures began before front-end logs appeared, indicating a deeper issue with service discovery.

evidence.description: [ERROR ×75] npm ERR! Failed at the microservices-demo-front-end@0.0.1 start script (front-end logs).
[ERROR ×40] dial tcp: lookup front-end on 127.0.0.11:53: read udp ... i/o timeout (edge-router logs, first at 16:23:23 UTC).
up{job="frontend"}: min=0.0, avg=0.70647, max=1.0 (peak at 16:14:51 UTC) (Metrics 05).
No traces for front-end between 16:13:21–16:24:00 UTC. First trace at 16:24:05 UTC shows DNS resolution failed (Trace Agent response).
http_requests_rate for front-end was 0 req/s for all status codes between 16:13:21–16:24:00 UTC (Metrics Agent response).

recommendations.action: Fix front-end startup configuration to resolve SyntaxError and npm ERR! failures. Validate environment variables and startup scripts.
recommendations.rationale: Directly addresses the root cause (configuration error) preventing front-end from starting.

recommendations.action: Add instrumentation for DNS server (127.0.0.11:53) to capture logs and metrics for resolution failures.
recommendations.rationale: Enables faster detection and diagnosis of DNS-related issues in the future.

recommendations.action: Implement health checks and auto-restart policies for front-end to recover from crashes automatically.
recommendations.rationale: Reduces mean time to recovery (MTTR) for front-end failures.

recommendations.action: Investigate orders service CPU throttling and latency spikes to optimize /metrics endpoint performance.
recommendations.rationale: Mitigates secondary impact on user experience during cascading failures.

recommendations.action: Review resource limits for front-end (memory) and orders (CPU) to prevent resource exhaustion.
recommendations.rationale: Reduces risk of crashes and throttling during high load.

impact: Users experienced a complete outage of the front-end service, resulting in failed requests, elevated 500 errors, and increased latency. The edge-router was unable to forward requests due to DNS resolution failures, and downstream services (e.g., orders) experienced secondary latency spikes.
